scope: CapSched-H production assurance case with Linux-only L0 prototype boundaries

status_legend.open: Required for the final goal but not established.
status_legend.model_supported: Supported by small or decomposed semantic models.
status_legend.prototype_evidenced: Supported by Linux compatibility or integration evidence only.
status_legend.protection_evidenced: Supported by monitor-backed production implementation and validation.
status_legend.forbidden_for_l0: Must not be claimed by Linux-only prototypes.

claims.title: Cross-Domain escape requires monitor or typed endpoint break
claims.claim: A Domain-local userspace and Linux-kernel-context compromise cannot cross into another Domain's memory, execution authority, device queue, DMA memory, or control authority without breaking the HyperTag Monitor or an explicitly exposed typed service endpoint.
gaps: No per-Domain mutable kernel state implementation.

claims.title: Non-forgeable Domain activation
claims.claim: Linux-visible Domain shadows do not create active authority; monitor validation of RunToken, epoch, placement, root budget, and MemoryView is required.
gaps: No arch-specific monitor root-budget timer implementation.
No proof that Linux cannot mutate active roots.

claims.title: No CPU execution without runnable authority
claims.claim: A task cannot enter or remain executable without a live RunCap-derived FrozenRunUse, SchedContext, epoch, generation, CPU placement, and remaining budget.
gaps: No runtime coverage for server-picked lower-class tasks.
No trace/runtime coverage for current, donor, and proxy execution paths.

claims.title: CPU and service execution stay within root budget
claims.claim: Domain CPU use and service execution on behalf of callers are bounded by non-forgeable root budgets and caller-reserved BudgetTickets.
gaps: No actual trace runtime coverage run under the N-138 contract.
No architecture-specific monitor timer interrupt/trap path.
No exact current/donor/proxy runtime charge equation across CFS, RT, deadline, sched_ext, hrtick, cgroup CPU time, and proxy execution.
No monitor root budget timer binding to server tickets.
No monitor root budget timer implementation.

claims.title: Resource access requires typed endpoint authority
claims.claim: Resource operations require EndpointCap-derived frozen use; scheduler RunCap is not resource authority.

claims.title: Async work preserves caller provenance
claims.claim: Domain-derived async work executes with caller provenance and frozen caller authority.
gaps: No timer, RCU, softirq, or completion provenance policy.

claims.title: Other Domain memory and mutable kernel state are unmapped
claims.claim: Domain-private user pages and mutable kernel pages are mapped only in the owning MemoryView or through explicit service/shared buffers.
gaps: No per-Domain mutable kernel-state allocator plan.

claims.title: No stale translation crosses activation or revoke
claims.claim: Domain activation and page revoke leave no usable stale direct-map, stage-2/EPT, ASID/PCID/VMID, IOMMU, or CPU TLB translation into another Domain.

claims.title: Mutable page cache is Domain-owned or service-mediated
claims.claim: Sealed base content may be shared, but mutable overlays are Domain-owned and commits are serialized through a service endpoint or equivalent token.

claims.title: Queue submit, DMA, IRQ, and rate revoke as one lease
claims.claim: Device queue ownership, allowed DMA memory, interrupt route, epoch, and queue budget/rate limit are one monitor-owned QueueLease boundary.
gaps: No monitor-backed QueueTag, QueueControlCap, RepresentorForwardCap, typed SubmitLedger, or DescriptorLedger.
No VF identity epoch and reassignment handoff implementation.
No HyperTag monitor interface or service/driver Domain split implementation.
No observation-only modern NIC HyperTag probe/stub Linux patch.
No root-management/local monitor LocalDomainDeviceLease compilation implementation.

claims.title: Modern NIC queue identity and binding
claims.claim: Queue effects require live QueueTag, queue epoch, ring, q_vector, NAPI instance, IRQ route, and queue budget.
gaps: No queue epoch emission or check.
No VF epoch or VSI generation emission/check.
No HyperTag monitor receipt minting implementation.
No queue budget root below Linux.

claims.title: Typed modern NIC submit classes
claims.claim: SKB, XDP frame, XDP_TX page-pool, and AF_XDP zero-copy submits are distinct authority classes.
gaps: No Domain/epoch correlation on packet memory.
No frozen XDP or AF_XDP descriptor authority.

claims.title: Descriptor publication and doorbell ledger
claims.claim: Descriptor writes, next_to_watch publication, in-flight DMA state, and tail doorbell publication consume a typed DescriptorLedger.
gaps: No stale descriptor quarantine after revoke.

claims.title: DMA packet memory ownership
claims.claim: DMA-capable packet memory requires MemoryView/IOMMU ownership and class-specific memory authority.
gaps: No monitor-backed MemoryView for packet pages.
No IOMMU ownership root below Linux.
No real monitor-backed DMA invalidation receipt implementation.
No real IOMMU invalidation latency or ordering proof.
No packet generation reset or retag implementation.
No VF mailbox QueueConfigCarrier or DMA ring-address carrier implementation.
No VF epoch handoff DMA/IOMMU receipt implementation.

claims.title: Modern NIC completion settlement
claims.claim: NAPI, IRQ, worker, or service completion settles a previously typed ledger under service authority and service budget.
gaps: No service BudgetTicket or service budget rule.
No proof that completion cannot deliver after revoke.
No stale XSK completion quarantine implementation.

claims.title: Modern NIC QueueControl authority
claims.claim: Devlink rate/scheduler and VF/SF/representor lifecycle operations require QueueControl authority and a live queue epoch.
gaps: No TC/switchdev OffloadCap or rule-generation proof.
No VF mailbox QueueControl carrier implementation.
No VF mailbox queue budget/quanta carrier implementation.
No VF epoch handoff QueueControl reopen proof.

claims.title: Modern NIC RepresentorForward authority
claims.claim: Representor transmit requires RepresentorForward authority plus a live lower QueueLease, fresh lower queue epoch, and service budget.
gaps: No fresh lower queue epoch check.
No metadata_dst generation or lower_dev-to-QueueLease binding.

claims.title: Modern NIC service work and async provenance
claims.claim: Reset, PTP, DPLL, eswitch, LAG, DIM, firmware, and maintenance work execute as service/kernel authority classes and cannot create caller-attributed effects without typed carriers.
gaps: No merge policy for coalesced work.
No cancellation/quarantine rule for stale queued work.
No frozen VF request carrier propagation into async FDIR completion.
No VF epoch handoff carrier for reset/reassignment reopen.

claims.title: Modern NIC queue revoke semantics
claims.claim: Queue revoke invalidates submit, descriptor, doorbell, in-flight DMA, completion, control, representor, and service work before authority can be reused or delivered to another Domain.
gaps: No monitor-backed IRQ route invalidation receipt implementation.
No monitor-backed DMA/IOMMU/MemoryView invalidation receipt implementation.
No stale XSK/page-pool quarantine or packet generation reset implementation.
No representor lower QueueLease invalidation or TC/offload stale-rule removal receipt implementation.
No service-work carrier merge policy proof.
No VF mailbox request carrier and async FDIR context implementation.
No VF identity epoch, VSI generation, mailbox embargo, or service replay handoff implementation.

claims.title: Modern NIC Linux substrate compatibility
claims.claim: Linux netdev, NAPI, XDP, AF_XDP, devlink, representor, workqueue, and tracepoint structures are useful compatibility substrates but not production authority roots.

claims.title: Epoch revoke invalidates all authority paths
claims.claim: Domain, process, object, queue, endpoint, and cluster epoch revocation invalidates active, queued, delayed, async, remote, and cached authority before reuse.

claims.title: Cluster leases compile local before use
claims.claim: Cluster-wide authority compiles into node-local Domain epoch, SchedContext, EndpointCap, BudgetTicket, and QueueLease state before local use.

claims.title: Linux compatibility is preserved
claims.claim: CapSched refines Linux scheduler, cgroup, cpuset, namespace, cred, LSM, rlimit, hotplug, and topology constraints without silently bypassing them or breaking ABI compatibility.
gaps: No runtime trace execution under the N-138 current/donor/proxy/server contract.

claims.title: Monitor and service domains stay smaller than VM/VMM attack area
claims.claim: The production TCB is small enough to improve or preserve attack surface while reducing cost versus per-VM guest kernels.

claims.title: Co-tenancy and side-channel policy is explicit
claims.claim: CPU core, SMT, cache, NUMA, device, and queue co-tenancy decisions are explicit Domain policy.

claims.title: Exploit and cost baselines are tested
claims.claim: CapSched-H provides VM-like cross-Domain protection and better cost efficiency for selected datacenter workloads.
gaps: No cost, tail-latency, throughput, or density benchmark.

evidence.limitation: Finite model gate checks TASK_WAKING freeze, donor/current/proxy budget collapse, and selected-state retry hazards; it is not Linux implementation, runtime coverage, monitor verification, or production protection evidence.
Finite model gate checks runtime charge subject classification; it is not Linux budget-hook implementation, runtime coverage, monitor timer evidence, or production protection evidence.
Full integration TLC run stopped after state explosion and is not a pass.
Finite model checks authority-class separation; it is not real NIC enforcement evidence.
Finite model checks XDP/AF_XDP memory ownership constraints; it is not real page-pool, XSK, UMEM, or IOMMU enforcement evidence.
Finite model checks QueueControl and RepresentorForward separation; it is not devlink, VF/SF, eswitch, or bridge policy correctness evidence.
Linux source anchors are mutable Linux substrate and not protection evidence.
Every readiness row is observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false.
Finite model checks revoke/drain/quarantine semantic constraints; it is not real queue reset, DMA drain, IRQ masking, IOMMU invalidation, or monitor enforcement evidence.
Maps ice shutdown/rebuild source anchors to formal revoke obligations; it is source-observed only and not protection evidence.

Every readiness row is observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, and monitor_verified=false; high-severity gaps remain for queue epoch, typed ledgers, IOMMU root, VF IRQ synchronization, XSK quarantine, representor lower lease, service carrier, and epoch handoff.
Finite model checks IRQ ownership separation for revoke; it is not MSI-X, interrupt-remapping, VFIO/iommufd, or monitor implementation evidence.
Finite model checks route invalidation receipt semantics across eventfd, Linux IRQ/MSI, IRTE/IEC flush, isolated MSI, and posted state; it is not monitor or hardware implementation evidence.
Maps ice, DMA API, IOMMU core, iommufd, VFIO, and arch-IOMMU invalidation substrate; it is source observation, not authority or protection evidence.
Finite model checks DMA/IOMMU/MemoryView invalidation receipt semantics; it is not real IOMMU, NIC, MemoryView, or monitor implementation evidence.
Maps XSK CQ submission, XSK free-list return, page-pool recycle, and packet memory return substrate; it is source observation, not authority or protection evidence.
Finite model checks stale XSK/page-pool completion quarantine and packet memory return semantics; it is not real AF_XDP, page-pool, ice, or monitor implementation evidence.
Maps ice representor, bridge/FDB/VLAN/switchdev, TC offload, BPF/TC redirect, and LAG lower_dev substrate; it is source observation, not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks representor lower QueueLease derivation, bridge/FDB/VLAN non-authority, TC/offload control authority, stale lower_dev rebind, forward-after-revoke, and representor-stop non-revoke semantics; it is not real bridge, TC, ice, or monitor implementation evidence.

Maps ice service task coalescing, reset/AdminQ/MailboxQ, VF virtchnl queue-control, PTP, DPLL, bridge/eswitch, LAG, GNSS, and reset/rebuild replay substrate; it is source observation, not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks service/caller authority intersection, per-effect carriers, coalesced service-loop merge hazards, PTP/DPLL control carriers, bridge/LAG/offload lower-lease requirements, and reset/rebuild replay freshness; it is not real driver or monitor enforcement evidence.

Maps ice VF mailbox queue config, VF-provided descriptor-ring DMA address, queue enable, IRQ map, queue bandwidth/quanta, FDIR offload, and FDIR async completion substrate; it is source observation, not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks VF mailbox request, QueueControl, DMA ring-address, IRQ route, queue budget, FDIR offload, async completion, and revoke carrier separation; it is not real virtchnl, ice, IOMMU, MSI-X, or monitor enforcement evidence.

Maps ice VF reset, teardown, MSI-X/vector remap, VSI generation, queue/DMA/IRQ config, FDIR async completion, mailbox dispatch, and service replay substrate; it is source observation, not authority or protection evidence.
Finite model checks VF id/VSI/queue/IRQ/DMA/FDIR/mailbox/service replay handoff freshness; it is not real SR-IOV, virtchnl, MSI-X, IOMMU, FDIR, or monitor enforcement evidence.

Maps the HyperTag Monitor, Linux service/driver Domain, target endpoint, receipt-minting, and data-plane trap boundary for modern NIC QueueLease/VF authority; it is architecture analysis, not implementation or protection evidence.

Finite model checks monitor-root, service-Domain, DMA/IRQ receipt, typed endpoint, local cluster-lease compilation, audit-only, and per-packet trap separation; it is not real monitor, driver, IOMMU, IRQ, or performance evidence.
Maps modern NIC HyperTag receipt/carrier rows to observation-only probes and inert stubs; it is implementation-readiness evidence only, not authority or protection evidence.

Finite model checks readiness-gate ordering and rejects behavior approval before gate satisfaction, probe-as-authority, non-inert stubs, missing coverage, raw endpoint stubs, and protection claims; it is not real Linux patch compatibility or monitor enforcement evidence.
Defines the observation-only source-anchor ledger and no-code runner shape for modern NIC HyperTag receipt/carrier rows; it is not authority or protection evidence.

Static runner emitted 37 ledger rows with 36 available source anchors, 1 expected LocalDomainDeviceLease external gap, and 0 safety flag violations; all rows remain observation_only=true, authority_claim=false, monitor_verified=false, behavior_change=false, and protection_claim=false.

Finite model checks local monitor lease compilation semantics and rejects remote ClusterLease direct use, scheduler placement as authority, service admission minting, Linux device registration authority, stale cluster epoch, wrong service/target Domains, queue receipt before local lease, and audit-only compile; it is not monitor, driver, IOMMU, IRQ, or protection evidence.

Finite model checks monitor-owned ring refinement constraints for slot authority, monitor claim, post-claim mutation, slot generation, batch epoch crossing, replay-before-ledger, response ownership, shadow source, revoke drain, and ring-full non-authority; it is not ABI layout or protection evidence.

Finite model checks combined-carrier constraints for monitor-owned attempt ids, shared replay, shared ledger, shared shadow generation, duplicate fallback success, response-without-ledger, epoch split, revoke across both carriers, and ring-full non-authority; it is not ABI layout, implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.

Defines implementation-facing direct-call carrier requirements for request envelope groups, bounded copy/freeze, replay key fields, ledger row semantics, response handle semantics, error classes, shared shadow generation, control lane behavior, and future ring compatibility; it is not binary ABI layout, implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.

Finite model checks direct-call carrier requirements for bounded copy-before-validation, carrier selection non-authority, replay-before-ledger, same-nonce digest mismatch, response ledger backing, shared shadow generation, timeout non-terminality, transport non-authority, control priority non-bypass, carrier sequence non-replay authority, and carrier-neutral namespaces; it is not binary ABI layout, implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.

Defines direct-call semantic schema compatibility rules for schema objects, negotiation acceptance, field criticality, canonical digest/replay binding, downgrade rejection, response/ledger/error schemas, and future ring compatibility; it is not numeric schema assignment, binary encoding, implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.

Finite model checks schema compatibility constraints for unsupported schema rejection, monitor/caller minimum downgrade rejection, mandatory/critical optional field handling, ignored optional non-authority, required feature coverage, safety-feature downgrade rejection, response/ledger compatibility, unknown success rejection, transport non-authority, and carrier-neutral schema namespace; it is not numeric schema assignment, binary encoding, implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.

Defines no-code direct-call Linux/monitor attachment rows, monitor responsibilities, readiness safety flags, inert stub constraints, failure-injection boundaries, and ring compatibility requirements; it is not a Linux patch, monitor implementation, binary ABI, user ABI, public tracepoint ABI, behavior change, or protection evidence.

Finite model checks no-code attachment-readiness constraints and rejects authority claims, monitor verification claims, behavior changes, user ABI, public tracepoint ABI, protection claims, authorizing or behavior-changing stubs, probe authority, Linux ledger/response minting, shadow refresh from timeout, live fault-injection effects, raw handle exposure, and direct-only ring incompatibility; it is not implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.

Runner emitted 41 source-only rows with 34 available anchors, 19 existing trace event declaration rows, 12 symbol candidate rows, 6 future gaps, 1 trace-plan row, 0 safety flag violations, and 41 overlay seed rows. It did not modify Linux, require root, write tracefs, attach probes, create public tracepoint ABI, or claim authority, monitor verification, runtime coverage, or protection; it is not ABI approval, monitor implementation, performance, liveness, or protection evidence.
Socket endpoint checks cannot rely only on LSM because some paths reuse sock_sendmsg_nosec().

Finite model gate checks server-borrow ticket and class runtime non-authority; it is not Linux scheduler-hook implementation, runtime coverage, monitor timer evidence, or production protection evidence.

Finite model gate checks monitor-owned root budget timer semantics and rejects Linux timers/accounting as authority; it is not a monitor implementation, Linux hook approval, runtime coverage, architecture-specific timer path, or production protection evidence.

counterexamples.meaning: Domain switch without flush or retag leaves stale TLB authority.
Per-overlay commit without base serialization permits stale commit.
IRQ aliasing across non-free queues must be forbidden.
Full ClusterLease integration TLC did not finish and is not a pass.
Unsafe scheduler authority refinements show expected counterexamples for TASK_WAKING before freeze, current-only proxy budget, run after retry, and run without class settlement.

Unsafe runtime charge models show expected counterexamples for unspecified charge, class runtime as root authority, proxy without ticket, remote-tick proxy authority, task_sched_runtime authority, and CFS proxy without donor/cgroup charge.

Unsafe server-ticket models show expected counterexamples for server pick without ticket, server runtime as root authority, RT bandwidth as root, SCX slice as authority, stale server epoch, stopped server with live run, and lower task without authority.

Unsafe runtime coverage models show expected counterexamples for missing current, missing donor, missing proxy relation, missing server coverage, missing evidence class, sched_stat_runtime as authority, remote tick as proxy coverage, trace coverage as protection, server lifecycle-only coverage, and class runtime as root budget evidence.

Unsafe monitor timer models show expected counterexamples for running without monitor timer, running without root budget, Linux timer as root authority, overrun after budget expiry, Linux charge as monitor charge, activation without sealed token, running after epoch revoke, running after monitor interrupt, NO_HZ stopping monitor timer, and protection claim without implementation.

forbidden_claims: A direct-call reference ABI sketch is a selected binary ABI, Linux stub, monitor implementation, performance result, or production protection proof.
A semantic ABI candidate is a selected binary ABI, monitor call layout, Linux stub, shared-ring format, or production protection proof.
BPF or sched_ext is the production root for No RunCap, no run.
Bridge FDB hit, VLAN allow, switchdev hwdom/offload mark, TC redirect target, or metadata_dst is lower QueueLease authority.
Choosing direct-call reference semantics means ring/batching is unnecessary for the datacenter performance target.
Cluster-wide leases are directly executable.
Coalesced service work can use a mutable last-caller carrier as authority.

Combined direct-call plus monitor-owned-ring carrier planning permits separate replay namespaces, carrier-local receipt ledgers, carrier-local shadow generations, duplicate fallback success, or one-carrier revoke completion.

Direct-call attachment readiness permits behavior change, user ABI, public tracepoint ABI, Linux ledger/response minting, Linux shadow refresh from timeout or return code, probe/stub authority, raw handle exposure, monitor verification claims, or protection claims.

Direct-call carrier requirements select a binary ABI, permit validation before bounded monitor copy/freeze, treat Linux timeout as monitor terminality, or create direct-only replay, ledger, or shadow namespaces.

Direct-call schema compatibility permits unsupported schema acceptance, downgrade of monitor/caller minimums, unknown mandatory fields, unknown success codes, transport observations as receipts, or direct-only schema namespaces.

Direct-call source inventory permits Linux modification, root requirement in source-only mode, tracefs writes in source-only mode, probe attachment, public tracepoint ABI creation, source/trace observations as authority, runtime observation claims, raw handle exposure, monitor verification claims, or protection claims.
EndpointCap is CPU execution authority.
FDIR ctx_irq, ctx_done, timer completion, IRQ completion, or service flush is frozen OffloadCap or VF request carrier authority.
IRQ route invalidation proves DMA reachability revocation.
LAG lower_dev rewrite preserves lower QueueLease authority without metadata and QueueLease rebind.
Linux hrtick, sched_tick, hrtimer, NO_HZ remote tick, task_sched_runtime, or Linux runtime charge reports are monitor root budget enforcement.
Linux netdev down/reset proves monitor-grade queue revoke.
Linux netdev, ring, q_vector, devlink, representor, tracepoint, or workqueue state is modern NIC QueueLease authority.
Linux page/slab/memcg/page-cache metadata is memory authority.
Linux service-domain queues, Linux-owned response stores, Linux-visible shadows, audit logs, ring slots, or raw driver handles are monitor receipt authority.
Linux-only L0 provides hypervisor-grade isolation.
Monitor-owned ring refinement permits Linux ring slots, Linux response publication, Linux-only slot generations, or ring-full/drop accounting to become authority.
Packet memory can be returned to a Domain after revoke without stale-completion quarantine and generation reset.
QoS caps, queue id checks, traffic class checks, or queue rate fields are queue budget authority.
Queue reassignment, packet page return, or new MemoryView mapping may occur before a DMA/IOMMU/MemoryView invalidation receipt.
QueueControl can be authorized by RunCap, CAP_NET_ADMIN alone, or plain netdev reachability.
Representor netdev queue stop proves lower QueueLease revoke.
Representor netdev reachability is lower QueueLease authority.
Ring batching can weaken monitor-owned response, replay, receipt ledger, revoke, or shadow invalidation semantics.
Root-management ClusterLease text, scheduler placement, service-domain admission, Linux PCI/devlink/IOMMU registration, or tracefs observation is a LocalDomainDeviceLease.
RunCap is resource endpoint authority.
Runtime trace coverage, sched_stat_runtime, sched_switch, remote tick, or DL server lifecycle trace is monitor root budget enforcement or production protection evidence.
SKB, XDP frame, XDP_TX page-pool, AF_XDP zero-copy, QueueControl, RepresentorForward, and ServiceWork can be collapsed into one queue capability.
SchedContext budget is monitor root budget.
TLC completion alone proves the datacenter OS goal.
VF mailbox queue/control effects can be authorized by virtchnl validation without a VF/Domain request carrier, DMA MemoryView proof, IRQ route proof, and QueueLease or QueueControl epoch.
VF-provided dma_ring_addr plus queue/ring validation is DMA MemoryView authority.
VFIO/iommufd Linux objects are production queue authority roots.
Vector id checks, q_vector lookup, or ice_cfg_interrupt success is monitor-backed IRQ route authority.

Worker identity, ICE_SERVICE_SCHED, virtchnl opcode allowlists, PTP/DPLL callback reachability, bridge/FDB events, LAG lower_dev rewrites, or reset/rebuild replay create service-work carrier authority.
XDP_TX or AF_XDP zero-copy can borrow SKB authority, generic netdev reachability, or ambient driver worker authority.
dma_unmap_*(), xsk_pool_dma_unmap(), iommufd IOAS unmap, VFIO unmap callback, iommu_unmap_fast(), queued IOVA flush, page unpin, or refcount release proves monitor-grade PageOwner transfer safety.
task_struct Domain pointers create a hard security boundary.
xsk_tx_completed(), xsk_buff_free(), page_pool recycle, or page_pool return proves stale packet memory can be normally returned after revoke.

gates.title: Vocabulary and build compatibility
gates.allowed_claim: CONFIG_CAPSCHED scaffolding and type-only vocabulary build without changing Linux scheduler behavior.

gates.title: Assurance case foundation
exit_criteria: Next Linux slice must name which claim it supports.

gates.title: Trace-only Linux observation
allowed: Domain shadow identity trace or debug counters.
Context-switch observation.
Wakeup/enqueue coverage measurement.
No scheduling decision changes.

gates.title: Modern NIC HyperTag readiness gate
allowed: Observation-only probe or no-code trace runner design.
Inert type/stub scaffolding with no behavior change.
Machine-readable receipt/carrier coverage ledger.
Root-management/local monitor observation contract for LocalDomainDeviceLease fields.
No-behavior-change validator for LocalDomainDeviceLease contract dependencies and safety flags.
Admission-protocol model for failure and revoke ordering.
Pre-ABI monitor admission interface boundary model.
Pre-ABI carrier/storage choice gate model.
Semantic monitor ABI candidate model before carrier or binary layout selection.
Direct-call versus monitor-owned-ring implementation-facing sketch comparison.
Direct-call reference ABI sketch before binary layout or implementation.
Monitor-owned ring refinement sketch before binary layout or implementation.
Combined direct-call plus monitor-owned-ring carrier plan before binary layout or implementation.
Direct-call carrier requirements gate before binary layout or implementation.
Direct-call semantic schema compatibility gate before binary layout or implementation.
Direct-call no-code attachment readiness gate before Linux stub, monitor implementation, binary layout, user ABI, public tracepoint ABI, or protection claim.
Direct-call source-only inventory contract before runner implementation, tracefs execution, Linux stub, public tracepoint ABI, or protection claim.
Direct-call source-only inventory runner execution and broadening, preserving no-root, no-tracefs-write, no-probe, no-public-tracepoint-ABI, and no-protection-claim rules.
forbidden: Behavior-changing QueueLease, DMA, IRQ, VF, representor, offload, or service-work enforcement.
Probe or stub treated as authority.
Readiness evidence described as protection evidence.
ClusterLease, scheduler placement, service admission, Linux device registration, or tracefs observation treated as LocalDomainDeviceLease authority.
Direct-call attachment rows, trace/source inventory, inert stubs, timeouts, or Linux-visible shadows treated as monitor authority, behavior approval, ABI selection, or protection evidence.
Source-only direct-call inventory treated as runtime coverage, tracefs execution, monitor verification, or permission to create public tracepoint ABI.
